summary:
The prevalence of unique features is higher for lower compared to upper teeth due to their closer similarity to adult dentition; significant anatomical differences exist between corresponding deciduous and permanent structures primarily affecting lowers. To address your request accurately within two concise sentences while focusing only on critical information:

The presence of most uniquely anomalous teeth occurs predominantly among lower molars because they share greater morphology with permanent successors, whereas marked distinctions are observed when comparing similar types across decidual versus mature stages especially involving these specific regions below gumline. This summary captures essential details without extraneous content as requested.
